text:
Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Holly who discovered the joy of exploring her big backyard. Every evening after school, Holly would head out with her favorite stuffed bunny, Patch, sneakily nibble on some raspberries stolen from Mrs. Jenkins' garden, and race across the lawns toward the pond.

Holly had vivid dreams of becoming a famous forest botanist someday. She admired the way the leaves swayed softly in the gentle breeze, and she dreamed of discovering new plants that could sing or talk. Her favorite nocturnal visitor was Sir Wormington, the owlet, who always whispered secrets about the woods.

On one moonlit night, Holly heard a peculiar rustling. Curiosity got the better of her, and she cautiously approached a bush where two owl owl birds seemed to be conversing. One Owl whispered something about a forgotten treasure map and the other, Nightweasel, chuckled about how kids nowadays spent their nights dreaming about treasure instead of searching the skies
